title: Odyssey, Book 17: The Beggar at the Palace
description: Book 17 of Homer's Odyssey: The Beggar at the Palace.
introduction: Book 17 of the Odyssey: The Beggar at the Palace. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.text: Odysseus went to the palace as a beggar. His old dog Argos, lying on a dung heap, recognized him, wagged his tail, and died. The suitors abused and mocked the beggar. Antinous threw a footstool at him. Penelope asked to speak with the stranger.
